text: Carol kicking off the Horizon design system work. We're building a TypeScript component library with React. Every UI component will be documented in Storybook. The goal is a consistent design language across Horizon, the developer portal, and Zenith.

Skyler's Figma designs for Horizon v1 are approved. Clean dark interface, Kafka real-time indicators, transaction status badges for Atlas data. The design follows the principles in the Horizon design spec doc in Notion.

The Horizon color system is decided: dark background (#0A0A0A), primary white (#FFFFFF), accent green for active states, red for error states (Atlas transaction failures), yellow for pending (Phoenix processing states). Carol approved.

Tyler is building the Horizon TypeScript component library. We're using Radix UI primitives with our custom design tokens. All components are fully typed with TypeScript. Carol is reviewing the API design for each component.

The data visualization components for Prism are being designed by Skyler. Real-time Kafka event charts, Atlas transaction volume graphs, Orion prediction confidence gauges. All React components with TypeScript, using recharts under the hood.

UX review of the Atlas transaction detail view: users need to see source, destination, amount, timestamp, and the Kafka event ID that triggered it. Carol reviewed with Alice — the Postgres data model supports all these fields. Approved.

The Horizon notification design is done. Real-time Kafka-powered toast notifications for Atlas transaction events. Skyler built the design, Tyler implemented in TypeScript. Carol reviewed for accessibility and brand consistency.

Design decision: the Orion ML prediction panel in Horizon will show confidence scores and the top 3 contributing features. Dave provided the data model from the PyTorch model output. Skyler designed the visualization, Tyler built it.

The mobile view for Horizon is designed. Carol decided not to invest in a native app yet — the TypeScript PWA works well on mobile. Skyler optimized the Figma designs for 375px and 768px breakpoints. Tyler is implementing in React.

The Horizon v2 redesign kickoff. Skyler has the new Figma prototype ready. Key improvements: better Kafka real-time data visualization, cleaner Atlas transaction drill-down, Orion prediction integration in the main dashboard view.

The Zenith customer success portal design is starting. Skyler will design it in Figma first. It needs to show Atlas transaction health, Phoenix Kafka consumer lag, and Orion accuracy metrics. Clean enterprise aesthetic. Carol owns design direction.

Tyler has completed the Horizon Storybook documentation. All 47 React TypeScript components are documented with props, states, and usage examples. The Storybook is deployed on GCP and accessible to all Meridian engineers.

The Apex export UI is designed by Skyler. Simple wizard: select transaction type from Atlas, choose date range, pick format (CSV or Parquet), confirm export. The TypeScript form components use the shared Horizon design system.

Carol approved the Nexus API documentation design. The developer portal uses the Horizon design system with React TypeScript components. Avery's content is in. Frank needs to review the OAuth flow diagrams before we publish.

The Prism analytics dashboard design is finalized by Skyler. Real-time Kafka Streams data, Postgres aggregate charts, Orion prediction trend lines. All designed in Figma, implemented by Carol's team in React TypeScript. Q2 launch target.

The design system token update: we've added semantic tokens for Atlas transaction states (pending/processing/complete/failed) and Phoenix Kafka states (queued/processing/delivered). Tyler updated all TypeScript types to match.

User testing feedback for Horizon v2: users love the real-time Kafka updates but are confused by the Orion prediction confidence scores. Skyler is redesigning the prediction panel with clearer explanations. Dave will help with the ML terminology.

The international localization design for Horizon is approved for Q4. Carol chose i18next for the TypeScript internationalization library. Skyler is designing the RTL layout for Arabic markets. The React components need to be refactored for RTL.

Tyler completed the Horizon feature flag UI. TypeScript React components with real-time Redis-backed feature flag state. When Carol toggles a feature in Horizon, it propagates via Kafka events to all connected users instantly.

The onboarding wizard for Horizon is designed and implemented. Skyler built the Figma prototype, Tyler built the TypeScript implementation. The wizard walks users through connecting an Atlas data source, configuring Phoenix events, and viewing their first Orion prediction.
